Please do the following: list all branches in the repo, then get the 30 most recent commits on the default branch (main or master — whichever exists). Next, retrieve the full details of that specific commit to see which files it changed. Then, look up the current open issues in the `storyteller` repository on GitHub (owner: `smoores-dev`, repo: `storyteller`) — fetch up to 50 issues — and find the issue number of the open issue with the highest number of comments. Finally, fetch the comments for that specific issue and report: (1) the SHA of the 'fix' commit you identified, (2) the total number of files changed in that commit, and (3) the body text of the first comment on the highest-commented open issue.
